commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Keep this in human review because the remaining blockers are real/replayed behavior proof and maintainer risk acceptance, not a narrow automated repair.; Cleared: The diff is confined to diagnostic logging/session classification code and tests; no concrete security or supply-chain concern was found. (sha=f48c10689495bc4bf35f0a4a315c7d6060dbbe81)
commands.review_summary:
The PR resolves app-agent diagnostic transcript files, records a transcript byte cursor when a session enters processing, and uses post-cursor assistant transcript text when classifying stalled or stuck sessions.

PR surface: Source +93, Tests +211. Total +304 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Source inspection gives a clear partial reproduction: current main cannot resolve non-cron app-agent transcript context from agent-scoped keys, but no live or replayed app-agent diagnostic log reproduction was supplied.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Keep this in human review because the remaining blockers are real/replayed behavior proof and maintainer risk acceptance, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(logging): resolve app-agent diagnostic transcripts
target.body:
## Summary
- Gate transcript-backed stalled classification on assistant output appended after the current processing turn starts.
- Keep reused app-agent session transcripts visible in diagnostics without letting old assistant output suppress recovery for a newly stuck turn.
- Add reused-session regression coverage plus cursor-level transcript reader coverage.

Fixes #91505

## Root cause and fix classification
- Root cause: Diagnostic attention classification treated any resolved `lastAssistant` transcript text as proof of active assistant progress, even when that assistant line came from a previous app-agent session turn. That reused-session state bug let old transcript context classify a new stale turn as `stalled_agent_run` with `recovery=none`.
- Why this is root-cause fix: The patch records the transcript byte cursor when a session enters `processing` and passes that cursor into transcript lookup for attention classification, so only assistant output appended after the current turn starts can disable recovery. Old transcript text remains available as diagnostic context, but the recovery decision now uses the current-turn state boundary instead of a stale session-wide transcript tail.
- Fix classification: Root-cause fix for diagnostic session transcript recovery gating.
- Maintainer-ready confidence: High for the local diagnostic-code regression scope. Live app-agent/OAuth/private-provider proof is explicitly unavailable in this environment and is not claimed.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: Reused app-agent transcript context no longer suppresses stuck-session recovery unless the transcript proves assistant output was appended during the current processing turn.
- Real environment tested: Local Linux worktree at `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91600`, using production diagnostic session context, diagnostic attention classification, heartbeat recovery, and stuck-session recovery test paths.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91600
PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec vitest run src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts
```
- Evidence after fix:
```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91600

Test Files  4 passed (4)
     Tests  108 passed (108)
  Start at  18:29:02
  Duration  6.12s (transform 2.44s, setup 380ms, import 4.67s, tests 506ms, environment 0ms)
```
- Observed result after fix: The focused test run passes. The reused-session regression now classifies old assistant transcript context as recoverable `stale_session_state` with `recovery=checking`, while a current-turn appended assistant message still produces `stalled_agent_run` with `recovery=none`.
- What was not tested: No live OAuth/private-provider session, private CI, or external app-agent production environment was available from this Linux machine. The submitted proof is local production-code regression coverage for the diagnostic paths that ClawSweeper flagged, and it does not claim live external-provider behavior.

## Regression test plan
- Target test file: `src/logging/diagnostic-session-context.test.ts`, `src/logging/diagnostic-session-attention.test.ts`, `src/logging/diagnostic.test.ts`, and `src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: A reused app-agent session with old assistant transcript output remains recovery-eligible for a new stuck turn, while assistant output appended after the processing cursor still counts as current-turn progress and suppresses recovery.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The tests lock the transcript cursor reader, the attention classifier, and the heartbeat integration path that emitted the wrong `recovery=none` decision, without adding unrelated provider mocks or changing live OAuth behavior.

## Scope and architecture boundaries
- What did NOT change: This does not change provider execution, OAuth auth flow, credential handling, transcript persistence format, session lane ownership, or stuck-session recovery execution. It only changes the diagnostic recovery decision to use a current-turn transcript cursor.
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: Diagnostic session state remains the source-of-truth for the active processing turn, and transcript files remain the source-of-truth for assistant transcript text. The patch links those two existing state boundaries with a byte cursor instead of introducing a new session schema or provider-specific override.

## Review findings addressed
- RF-001: The PR was in `status: waiting on author`; this update addresses the current author-side blocker and provides current-head local evidence for re-review.
- RF-002: Transcript gating now requires current-turn assistant transcript output instead of any old assistant output from a reused session.
- RF-003: Added a reused-session regression that proves old assistant text remains recoverable for a new stuck turn.
- RF-004: The stale recovery suppression risk is covered by the regression that expects `classification=stale_session_state` and `recovery=checking` when only old assistant transcript context exists.
- RF-005: The repair is narrow: transcript-backed classification disables recovery only when the cursor proves current-turn progress.
- RF-006: `src/logging/diagnostic.ts` now passes `assistantAfterByteOffset` from session state into diagnostic context resolution and uses `currentTurnAssistant` for recovery gating.
- RF-007: Focused context transcript tests were run after the patch on the current head.
- RF-008: Focused attention classification tests were run after the patch on the current head.
- RF-009: Focused diagnostic heartbeat/recovery tests were run after the patch on the current head.
- RF-010: The daily-fix commit/verification flow ran after formatting; the submitted diff has a fresh verification marker for the current head.

## Merge risk
- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk: session-state`, `merge-risk: security-boundary`, and `merge-risk: availability`.
- Risk explanation: Session-state risk is limited to diagnostic bookkeeping for the active transcript cursor and does not change transcript storage or session identity ownership. The security-boundary signal is the `parseAgentSessionKey` import, which only parses an existing session identity string to locate the diagnostic transcript file; it does not read credentials, authorize access, or change provider/auth enforcement. Availability risk is improved because stale sessions with only old transcript context can recover again.
- Why acceptable: The recovery decision is narrowed to current-turn evidence, old transcript context remains visible for logs, and no auth/provider execution path is widened. Focused regression coverage verifies both sides of the boundary: old assistant output no longer blocks recovery, and current-turn assistant output still prevents unnecessary recovery.
- Patch quality notes: The patch-quality warnings about `return undefined` and `return 0` are diagnostic default returns for absent transcript files or cursor ranges, not production fallback masking. The cursor default of zero means an absent transcript starts with no assistant progress, and the security/session-state merge-risk signals are explained above.

## Maintainer-ready assessment
- Why it matters / User impact: A reused app-agent/OAuth session can otherwise leave a genuinely stuck new turn unrecovered just because an old assistant line exists in the transcript. This fix restores recovery for that user-visible stuck state while preserving useful transcript context for diagnosis.
